# Transcrição: It's_Time_to_Revisit_Creative_Testing

- **Fonte:** It's_Time_to_Revisit_Creative_Testing
- **Duração:** 09:57
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:06]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. It's time to revisit creative testing. Let's go. So this is a topic I've been thinking about a lot lately and honestly I've been struggling with how we test created for much of the past couple of years But I couldn't put my finger on what it was that bothered me so much Now I know that I disagree with the focus advertisers put on creative testing these days And I know that I don't do many of the things that other advertisers do it's felt like a waste of time and resources and I think I'm Finally able to clearly articulate why I've been doing this for a really long time and I admit That's not always an advantage It can be a disadvantage when you're stuck in your ways or you resist the rapid changes And look there is a get off my lawn element that I've had to work hard to avoid But the advantage is perspective So not only have I seen so much over the past 14 plus years But I've also done it and most importantly I've documented it So I've referred back to my blog so many times to confirm when something has changed or What things used to look like and how we treated ad creative is a big one So understand that things were so much simpler Back in the day and so I went back my old blog posts to confirm all this and the screenshots and everything It's just it's crazy prior to 2017 One ad was a very basic construction It consisted of one image or video one primary text one headline and one description And we couldn't even customize by placement yet And in fact you may not even realize just how simple the placements once were so going back to like 2013 or 14 it was just Facebook desktop Mobile and right column. That's it Each ad was a single combination of ad copy and creative One combination so you can imagine that creative testing back then was simply creating multiple ads To see what worked and you could actually isolate the best performing combination back then But things started to change at the end of 2017 now by then there were 12 placements and Facebook introduced dynamic creative which allowed us to provide up to 10 images or videos 5 primary text 5 headlines and 5 descriptions for a single ad that changed a lot and dynamic creative when you think about it really was the precursor of

## [03:06 - 06:08]

Today's ad creation because much of that has been adopted in some way now Finally in 2019 so we're still six years ago. We were able to customize creative by placement We couldn't do it until then if you wanted to create different creative by placement You had to create a separate ad set and add to do that So things were already much different then since one ad could have many different variations And now look at where we are today with creative. There are 26 different placements And we can customize both text and creative for all of them and actually met us to submit three different aspect ratios, but we could actually Find tune it even more if we wanted to and now the five primary text and five headlines Are baked into normal ad creation? In fact, you can even add up to an additional five AI generated primary text and headlines So think about this in the past there was just one possible combination of ad copy and creative for a single ad But now with all the different variables There are so many more if you turn on advanced plus creative enhancements and things like site links That customizes copy and creative even more and you can also accept AI generated images and background options The amount of customization to create new variations is insane So instead of having one possible combination of ad copy and creative it could easily be many hundreds or even many thousands of combinations So do you see where i'm going with this? It made sense back then to optimize your ads by finding the highest performing combination of ad copy and creative But now that's truly an impossible task. It's not only impossible, but it's unnecessary ads work differently now a different version of your ad will appear for different people Based on several factors. So based on the placement their device How they consume content or what meta thinks they're most likely to respond to So not only are many combinations necessary to get the best results, but the best ads will have many high performing combinations of ad copy and creative So here's a simple example That might help you understand why you don't need to isolate the best combination and then toss out the others people consume content completely differently on instagram reels and they do on Facebook desktop right column for example So hopefully you have a nine by sixteen video on reels and it's probably a square Static image on right column

## [06:09 - 09:14]

You're going to get completely different engagement and results from both and you'll probably get way more engagement from the video on reels But does that mean that the video version is better and is the quote unquote winner? Does that mean you should remove the static version that you're using right column and may use in other placements? Of course not a reels version will not work in right column But you have a version that would give you the best chance for results there So now consider the different ways that people consume content across all 26 placements and consider that every person consumes content differently and responds differently based on a type of media and text the many combinations are necessary There can't just be one That doesn't mean that you don't need to know what works You should still monitor which videos work best which images work best Which primary texts and headlines work best and you can apply what you learn to your next batch of ads But the days of going with one variation to optimize your ads and discarding the rest are over So here's the bottom of the glass I encourage you to question whether your method of creative testing is still necessary or helpful If you're creating separate campaigns or ad sets for this, why? What is it you're trying to accomplish and also consider that this can be counterproductive? Oxygen overlap is a real thing and you can make your results worse There's also no guarantee that the results you get from a test Will be the same as the results you get when you move those ads over So my preference is to keep this simple I don't run separate campaigns for testing when I create a new campaign I put all the ads in it that i'm going to need at that time if the results are good I don't touch anything if results could be better. I think about what different angles I could use for new ads And I know this is blasphemous to some advertisers I add those new ads to the existing campaign and ad set it will restart learning I don't care in some cases that may actually be good So whether you take this liberal approach to ad copy and creative is up to you But the main thing is that you should understand this very important point The success of your ads isn't based on finding the best combination of ad copy and creative You will need many high performing combinations of copy and creative to get good results For more on the evolution of copy and creative control, check out my recent blog post at johnlumber.com slash control Thanks again so much for joining me on the pubcast I really do appreciate it and if you're a regular or you want to become a regular There are a couple things you can do. So first up no matter where you are, whether it's apple podcast spotify
